title: Disable a line with a comment
prompt_md:
## Goal

The starter program is supposed to read two integers and print their **sum**, but a stray statement overwrites the result before it is printed. Disable that statement using a single-line comment (`//`) so the correct sum is printed.

Do not rewrite the math — just comment out the offending line.

## Input

Two integers on one line, separated by a space.

## Output

A single line: the sum of the two integers.

### Example

**Input:** `3 4`  
**Output:**
```
7
```

title: Switch behavior with comments
prompt_md:
## Goal

The program should read an integer and print its **square**. Right now an incorrect statement (which doubles the value) is active, while the correct statement sits commented out just below it. Use comments to disable the wrong line and enable the right one.

## Input

A single integer.

## Output

A single line: the square of the input.

### Example

**Input:** `3`  
**Output:**
```
9
```
